- The **dune** 3.24.0 release ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) prompted a round of follow-up maintenance PRs bounding or disabling brittle downstream tests ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161), [#30160](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30160), [#30187](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30187), [#30183](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30183))
latest_week.discussions_summary: Debate over who fixes dune-3.24-triggered revdep breakage, a simple_httpd checksum dispute, and OCaml 5.5.0 release-readiness tracking.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- Contributors debated ownership of revdep breakage following the dune 3.24.0 release, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asking whether fixing packages broken by a dune release falls to the opam-repository maintainers, the dune maintainers, or the affected package's own maintainer ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))
- [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) agreed to mark the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 as unavailable rather than resolve a disputed checksum, after [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) raised the discrepancy ([#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) tracked OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness ([#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463), closed), while a related bug where a second switch build of 5.5.0~alpha fails on macOS was reported by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) and traced to an upstream fix opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) asked the community to stop running `dune runtest` unconditionally in CI, with [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) noting it breaks tests tied to packages pending OCaml 5.5 support and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) pointing to an existing mechanism for running a subset of tests ([#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))
latest_week.trends_summary: Coordinated MirageOS releases, OCaml 5.5.0 release prep, and dune-3.24 revdep fallout dominated the week.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) pushed a coordinated round of MirageOS-ecosystem releases spanning x509, tls, charrua, mirage, and kdf within the same week ([#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154), [#30155](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30155), [#30156](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30156), [#30184](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30184), [#30195](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30195), [#30166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30166))
- The dune 3.24.0 release ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) triggered a wave of downstream compatibility PRs bounding dune versions or disabling brittle tests, surfacing an open question about who owns fixing revdep breakage ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161), [#30160](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30160))
- Preparation for the OCaml 5.5.0 release continued, with a release-readiness tracking issue ([#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)), a camlp5 release fixing a test broken by 5.5.0 ([#30163](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30163)), and the OCaml 5.4.0 manual package disabled over a bad checksum ([#30109](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30109))
- New package submissions touched on AI/agent tooling, including a "Programmable Agent Runtime" package ([#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)) and a property-based testing library with built-in Antithesis support ([#30185](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30185))
latest_week.contributors_summary: Package releases from hannesm, shonfeder, sim642, and others, plus review discussion on hegel, simple_httpd, and dune-3.24 fallout.
latest_week.contributors:

latest_week.brief_summary: New CSS Font Loading, Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache, and Web Worker toplevel bindings landed alongside a 6.4.1 dune-3.24 compatibility release.
latest_week.new_features_summary: CSS Font Loading, Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache API bindings, Web Worker toplevel, a tyxml attribute fix, and type-checked doc examples.
latest_week.new_features:
- Partial **CSS Font Loading API** binding added via a new `FontFace` module (`fontFace`/`fontFaceSet` class types, `FontFace.create`) plus a `fonts` property on `Dom_html.document`, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2255)), closing [#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1833) originally started by [Mesmera (WAFT)](https://github.com/mesmerizingwaft) and resolving [#66](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/66)-adjacent API-coverage requests
- New **Service Worker, MessageChannel, and Cache API** bindings by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2381)), including `serviceWorkerContainer`/`serviceWorkerRegistration`, the worker-side `serviceWorkerGlobalScope`, `MessageChannel`/`MessagePort`/`MessageEvent`, and `Cache`/`CacheStorage`, closing the long-standing feature request [#1464](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1464)
- **Toplevel `Wrapped` and `Async` APIs** plus a runnable **Web Worker toplevel** example landed by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2377)); `Wrapped` returns errors/warnings as first-class values instead of printing to stderr, and `Async` (new `js_of_ocaml-toplevel.lwt`-based sublibrary) drives the toplevel in a Web Worker via message passing — closing [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/833) and addressing [#66](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/66)
- Fixed **repeated-attribute handling in Tyxml_js** (e.g. multiple `a_class`) so the first occurrence wins and only it is reactively subscribed, matching browser DOM parsing semantics instead of leaving multiple live signals fighting over the same attribute, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2383](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2383)), fixing [#968](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/968) reported by [Clément Busschaert](https://github.com/clembu)
- Released **6.4.1**, a point release carrying only the dune-3.24 compatibility fixes (wat-module naming in `runtime/wasm/args.ml` and test path handling) on top of 6.4.0, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2386](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2386), [#2384](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2384))
- Added **type-checked documentation examples**: `{@ocaml[ … ]}` code blocks in `.mli` doc-comments and `.mld` manual pages are now compiled against the real library under `dune build @runtest` (with `parse`/`skip`/`prelude` opt-out markers), by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387)), closing [#1020](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1020) filed by [Olle Härstedt](https://github.com/olleharstedt); the checker caught and fixed several latent bugs in the manual and library doc-comments in the same PR
latest_week.activity_summary: Five new PRs opened this week (WebGL2, MessageEvent consolidation, wasm identifier renaming, a compare-op fix, wasm asset resolution) alongside merged wasm runtime clean-up.
latest_week.activity:
- Five **new PRs opened** this week and still under review: a draft **WebGL2 bindings** module by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) building on the existing WebGL binding ([#2388](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2388)), a draft consolidating five duplicate `messageEvent` class types into one shared `Dom_html.messageEvent` binding by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2390](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2390), fixing [#2382](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2382)), a rename of non-Rust-safe wasm `.wat` identifiers by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2392](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2392)), a fix restoring the immediate-vs-custom-block comparison path in `caml_compare_val` (needed for zarith-style custom blocks) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2391](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2391)), and a fix resolving wasm/WASI runtime assets relative to the runtime file instead of `require.main` by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2389](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2389))
- **Wasm runtime clean-up** merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2372)), touching 37 files as part of an ongoing migration of the `.wat` runtime sources toward the **Wax** authoring tool, with a further draft PR to switch runtime files to Wax outstanding ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2373))
- Manual **sidebar ordering** for the Ocsigen documentation site continues as an open PR from [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat), adding `@children_order` so odoc lists manual pages in reading order rather than alphabetically ([#2359](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2359))
- A **compiler `subst` speedup** PR by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) was closed without merging ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2185)), as was the draft **initial OCaml 5.5 support** PR touching 72 files ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2195)), and the superseded WIP toplevel interface ([#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/833)) and original CSS Font Loading attempt ([#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1833)) were both closed in favor of their successor PRs
- A long-pending PR to **consume compiler hints** produced by [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/13438) was merged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) after CI review ([#1721](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1721))
- Several older **wasm-focused PRs** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) remain open and were flagged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) for a triage decision: more precise function/environment types ([#1908](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1908)), more precise Wasm types generally ([#1907](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1907)), an option to back OCaml strings with JavaScript strings ([#1772](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1772)), and embedding Wasm code inside the JavaScript script ([#1758](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1758))
latest_week.discussions_summary: Reviewers discussed zarith custom-block comparison semantics and whether the new doc-example checker should move upstream into odoc.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- On the **compare-op regression fix** [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raised open questions about why the zarith-related regression wasn't caught by existing tests, whether Jane Street tests should run under JS, and how the fix interacts with the wasm backend ([#2391](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2391))
- On the **type-checked doc examples** PR, [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) suggested the checking mechanism could eventually be upstreamed into `wodoc` or `odoc` itself, with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) pointing to a related upstream discussion in [ocaml/odoc#303](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/303) ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387))
- On the **wasm asset resolution fix**, [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) suggested relocating the fix's test to `tests-wasm_of_ocaml` since it only exercises wasm-specific behavior ([#2389](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2389))
latest_week.trends_summary: Continued expansion of browser API bindings and an ongoing migration of the wasm runtime sources to the Wax authoring tool.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- Steady **expansion of Web Platform API coverage**: CSS Font Loading ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2255)) and Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2381)) landed this week, with WebGL2 ([#2388](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2388)) and a MessageEvent-type consolidation ([#2390](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2390)) already in draft
- Ongoing migration of the **wasm runtime `.wat` sources** toward the Wax authoring tool, visible in the identifier-renaming work needed for Rust-compatible names ([#2392](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2392)), the runtime clean-up ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2372)), and the outstanding switch-to-Wax draft ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2373))
- Investment in **documentation reliability tooling**, with the new type-checked example-block system ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387)) immediately surfacing and fixing several latent doc-comment bugs across the manual and library interfaces
latest_week.contributors_summary: Two contributors were active in code this week, alongside issue reporters and reviewers who shaped the merged work.

- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged the CSS Font Loading API binding ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2255)), Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache bindings ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2381)), the toplevel Wrapped/Async APIs ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2377)), the tyxml attribute fix ([#2383](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2383)), the type-checked doc examples system ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387)), and the ocaml#13438 hints PR ([#1721](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1721)); managed the 6.4.1 release ([#2386](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2386), [#2384](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2384)); opened draft PRs for WebGL2 bindings ([#2388](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2388)) and MessageEvent consolidation ([#2390](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2390)); closed several superseded/stale PRs ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2185), [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2195), [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/833), [#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1833)); and reviewed/commented on [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s compare-op fix and wasm asset resolution PRs ([#2391](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2391), [#2389](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2389))

oldest_week.new_features_summary: Major architecture change removing wasm backend, improved sourcemap index support, and modernized runtime features
oldest_week.new_features:
- **Complete removal of wasm_of_ocaml backend** (~32,000 lines of WASM-related code deleted), transforming the project back to pure JavaScript compilation ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
- **Enhanced sourcemap support** with index mapping for linking multiple JS files together, improving debugging capabilities ([1e54c94](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/1e54c943918016553ed2ba6cf75abe29596b8833), [a0a03bd](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/a0a03bd67ecdf83a86350b9e5b391ceb39c9cf72), [6f38333](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/6f38333b2e704a615afd7dca0aab101f68052f73))
- **Modern JavaScript runtime optimization** using static `Object.hasOwn` instead of dynamic property checks for better performance ([1bc3ab8](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/1bc3ab8cde58915a35b4f2af59fae9c27f8dc392))
- **Improved weak reference handling** with complete runtime reimplementation for better memory management ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
- **Global dead code elimination regression tests** added to prevent future issues ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067), [ebc6c4c](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/ebc6c4c00ccfede2fff6890e2f9c81b8b91bf5a8))
oldest_week.activity_summary: Major architectural shift with extensive code removal and new feature request for Dom_html modernization
oldest_week.activity:
- **Complete architectural transformation** removing all WebAssembly compilation support in favor of pure JavaScript target, affecting compiler, runtime, and build systems ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
- **Documentation rewrite** completely updating README from wasm_of_ocaml focus to comprehensive js_of_ocaml documentation with installation guides, feature descriptions, and data representation details ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
- **Runtime system modernization** across multiple JavaScript modules including improvements to weak references, bigarray handling, and parsing functions ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
- **Build system cleanup** removing wasm-related build configurations, test suites, and GitHub workflow files while updating opam package specifications ([4cd7df4](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4cd7df45183ca6f590240eeb6f48c984fb925067))
oldest_week.trends_summary: Major simplification trend moving from dual JS/WASM targets to single JavaScript focus
oldest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Backend consolidation strategy** with the project moving away from dual compilation targets (JavaScript + WebAssembly) to focus exclusively on JavaScript compilation, simplifying maintenance and development efforts
- **Modern JavaScript adoption** with runtime optimizations using current browser APIs like `Object.hasOwn` for improved performance and compatibility
- **Enhanced debugging capabilities** through improved sourcemap handling, particularly for complex linking scenarios involving multiple JavaScript files
oldest_week.contributors_summary: Limited activity with one feature request by Jérôme Vouillon and ongoing experimental work by hhugo
oldest_week.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened feature request [#1719](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1719) for auditing and modernizing Dom_html module, focusing on updating mouse event properties from int to float and removing outdated Internet Explorer compatibility code
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) continues experimental work on monomorphic representation for MlBytes in [#1229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1229), an ongoing draft PR from 2022 focused on runtime performance optimizations using deoptigate analysis

latest_week.brief_summary: Added `array#`/`iarray#` unboxed types and external pointer primitives, fixed a memory corruption bug in ephemerons, and bumped the base to OCaml 5.4
latest_week.new_features_summary: New unboxed `array#`/`iarray#` types, external pointer primitives, ELF notes for magic-trace, and several runtime/type-checker correctness fixes
latest_week.new_features:
- Added unboxed **`array#` and `iarray#`** types of kind `any`, a step towards using boxed-index-free array representations, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6361](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6361))
- Added **external pointer primitives** `%unsafe_set_ext_ptr`, `%unsafe_get_ext_ptr`, and `%unsafe_get_ext_ptr_imm` for libraries building off-heap single-word pointers without base+offset arithmetic, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6350))
- Extended **scannable axis** support to arbitrary and abstract kinds and fixed/enabled the associated warnings, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6367](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6367), [#6360](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6360), [#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874))
- Compiler hint reporting now emits **printed warnings instead of fatal errors**, avoiding hard failures for non-critical diagnostics, by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#6275](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6275))
- Added an all-void variant constructor **attribute requirement** to catch a class of unboxed-type mistakes at compile time, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256))
- Merged the **redundant modality warning** onto main, flagging module-level mode/modality annotations that are already implied, by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5532](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5532))
- Fixed a **hard-to-trigger memory corruption bug in ephemerons** by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#6323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6323)), plus related minor-GC correctness fixes for atomic release-store forwarding pointers ([#6330](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6330)) and continuation callstack tagging ([#6327](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6327))
- Fixed an **unaligned 128-bit load/store address computation** on arm64 by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#6388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6388)), and ensured the `Poll` CFG operation carries empty `arg`/`res` by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#6326](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6326))
- Fixed **O(N²) data structures in the linker's module tracking**, improving link-time scaling for large programs, by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) ([#5807](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5807))
- Marked additional flambda2 functions `[@inline]` to reduce **compilation time**, by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6376))
- Added an `ocaml_eff` **ELF notes section** recording effect-implementation branch offsets, replacing hard-coded offsets previously needed by the `magic-trace` tool, by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#6274](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6274))
- Bumped the compiler **magic number for the 5.4.0-ox2 runtime** by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#6310](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6310)), and updated `README.md`/`HACKING.md` opam switch instructions to reference the **OCaml 5.4 base** ([2a13fe0](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/commit/2a13fe094d297d6ed4934e10a3412e455ab442c5))
- Updated **kind syntax documentation** to match recent scannable-axes and abstract-kind changes, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6353](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6353))
latest_week.activity_summary: 27 PRs opened this week remain under review, including a Merlin type-recovery integration and AVX512 intrinsics, alongside ongoing SSA-backend and fiber-dynamics work
latest_week.activity:
- 27 **PRs opened** this week are still under review, spanning a vectorizer bug reproduction and CFG-invariant strengthening by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#6368](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6368), [#6369](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6369), [#6370](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6370), [#6371](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6371), [#6372](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6372)), AVX512 intrinsics and dynamic-binding work from [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#6378](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6378), [#6380](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6380), [#6381](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6381)), and several jkind/typing fixes from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6395](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6395), [#6406](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6406))
- A `datalog` allocation-avoidance change for the reaper's rule evaluation was opened by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6377](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6377)), alongside his merged fixes for flambda2 join environments and switch-argument equations ([#6348](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6348), [#6349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6349), [#6373](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6373), [#4394](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4394))
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened work to add a **Merlin typing-recovery mechanism** directly into the type checker ([#6366](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6366)), and [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) opened **quoted areality** and **heap-to-stack pointer invalidation** work for flambda2, reviewed by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([#6404](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6404), [#6400](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6400))
- The in-progress **SSA intermediate representation between Cmm and CFG** from [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) continues under review from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), with a related merged fix aligning minor CFG behavior with SSA ([#6397](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6397))
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat)'s **fiber dynamic-binding table** redesign remains under detailed review from [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([#6359](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6359)), alongside his merged simplification removing the dynamic root value ([#6357](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6357)) and debug-mode-only stack-mapping naming ([#6280](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6280))
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)'s **dynamic staticity for modules without a guaranteed cmx** continues under review from [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([#6226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6226)), while a related functorization PR handling circular aliases was opened and closed the same week ([#6385](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6385))
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) merged two reaper/flambda2 fixes for duplicated lifted constants during continuation specialization and a fatal error during unboxing ([#6398](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6398), [#6308](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6308))
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) merged eight runtime fixes covering thread-tick division guards, zombie-thread ring handling, preemption continuations, and dynamic-binding cache flushing across fibers ([#6329](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6329), [#6345](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6345), [#6333](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6333), [#6341](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6341), [#6331](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6331)), and opened a frametable-size reduction PR ([#6399](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6399))
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged a mode-crossing fix for `any` fields in principal toplevel ([#6386](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6386)) and has several typing/warning PRs open, including a redundant-modifier warning under review from [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#5436](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5436)) and functor modality interactions ([#5371](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5371))
- [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) merged codegen-test infrastructure cleanup ([#6375](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6375), [#6232](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6232)) and opened automated `expect_asm` test generation for scalar primitives ([#6390](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6390))
latest_week.discussions_summary: Extended review threads on the SSA backend, fiber dynamic-binding tables, and dynamic staticity PRs surfaced design disagreements over comment style and layout
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- Review of [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)'s SSA intermediate representation ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) drew close scrutiny from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) over apparent regressions and warning-suppression choices, spanning nearly 40 review comments
- [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)'s review of the fiber dynamic-binding table redesign ([#6359](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6359)) pushed for clearer invariant documentation, better grep-ability of stack-context word counts, and reconsideration of struct layout for `stack_info`
- Reviewers on [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)'s dynamic-staticity PR ([#6226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6226)) including [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) pushed back on AI-generated comment style in the code, calling it inconsistent with the codebase's conventions and often unhelpful
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) requested that the redundant-modifier warning ([#5436](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5436)) explain *why* a modifier is redundant (e.g. "because it is implied by global") rather than just flagging it, and questioned the non-uniform treatment across tests
- Reviewers on the `alloc_regions` plumbing PR ([#6121](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6121)) including [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) probed edge cases around ghost regions and requested expanded explanatory comments over inline `XXX` markers
latest_week.trends_summary: Continued focus on unboxed-types/kind-system refinement, runtime correctness hardening by a single maintainer, and flambda2/reaper bottom-environment cleanup
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- Continued incremental expansion of the **unboxed types and kind system**, with scannable axes, `array#`/`iarray#`, and all-void variant attribute requirements landing in the same week from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6361](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6361), [#6367](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6367), [#6360](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6360), [#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874), [#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256))
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) is systematically hardening the **multicore runtime**, merging eight distinct fixes this week touching ephemerons, minor GC forwarding pointers, thread ticks, and fiber dynamic-binding caches ([#6323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6323), [#6330](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6330), [#6327](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6327), [#6329](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6329), [#6331](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6331), [#6333](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6333), [#6341](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6341), [#6345](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6345))
- Flambda2/reaper work is converging on eliminating **"bottom" environments** during join and simplification, with three related merges from [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6348](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6348), [#6349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6349)) and continued discussion in his open BFS meet-strategy PR ([#6191](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6191))
- Several open PRs from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) target **vectorizer and CFG-layout correctness** ([#6368](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6368), [#6369](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6369), [#6370](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6370), [#6371](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6371), [#6372](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6372)), suggesting an active push to strengthen backend invariants ahead of further vectorization work

latest_week.brief_summary: Release v2.15.0 shipped with per-operation RWF flags and a liburing 2.15 update, plus fixes for opam sandbox builds and Eio compatibility.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Per-operation RWF flags for read/write ops and vendored liburing bumped to 2.15, released as v2.15.0.
latest_week.new_features:
- New `Rw_flags` module exposes per-operation `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (`hipri`, `dsync`, `sync`, `nowait`, `append`, `noappend`, `atomic`, `dontcache`) and threads an optional `?flags` argument through `read`, `write`, `readv`, `writev`, `read_fixed`, `read_chunk`, `write_fixed` and `write_chunk`, letting callers request per-write durability (`dsync`/`sync`) or drop pages from the cache after streaming I/O (`dontcache`) without a separate `fsync` call, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154))
- Vendored `liburing` updated to 2.15, pulling in a new `bpf_filter.h` header and the corresponding `liburing.so.2.15` shared library into the `dune` build rules, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
- `FLAGS.t` restored to `private int` (rather than made fully abstract) so that existing code depending on the underlying integer representation, such as Eio, keeps working ([ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)), by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159))
- Fixed the vendored `configure` test to avoid writing to `/tmp`, which opam's build sandbox marks read-only and had broken installation of `uring.dev`, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158))
- Release [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) published, bundling this week's `Rw_flags` and liburing 2.15 work together with earlier accumulated new ops and bug fixes, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- `README.md` subtree-pull instructions updated to reference the `liburing-2.15` tag, and `tests/main.md` gained a worked example showing `dsync` writes, flag-free `readv`, and the `EOPNOTSUPP` fallback path when an unsupported flag bit is set, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
latest_week.activity_summary: Three PRs opened and merged within the week for build sandboxing and FLAGS.t compatibility, alongside the RWF flags work and the v2.15.0 release.
latest_week.activity:
- All three PRs opened this week were merged the same week: the liburing 2.15 update by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), followed in quick succession by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s opam-sandbox build fix ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)) and `FLAGS.t` compatibility revert ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), both merged within a day of opening.
- The per-operation `RWF` flags PR opened in the prior week by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) was merged this week ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), rounding out the API work that fed into the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release published by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm).
- Discussion continued on the open issue about building without vendored `liburing`, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explaining the opam-distribution rationale and exploring an optional environment-variable toggle for a future version ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
latest_week.discussions_summary: Ongoing debate on issue #156 over vendoring liburing versus depending on a system-provided version.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) argued against vendoring `liburing` and asked for a way to build against the system library, citing a Debian 13 packaging mismatch on the minimum required version; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded that vendoring is important for opam distribution while `liburing` remains unavailable on several distros, noted he explored an optional environment-variable toggle to decouple it but found it messy, and agreed with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s suggestion that a packaging-layer patch is the best near-term fix ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
- Review on the liburing 2.15 update surfaced an opam sandbox build failure (`mktemp` failing against a read-only `/tmp`) caught by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) shortly after merge, resolved via a follow-up fix that was folded into the same buildable history ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
latest_week.trends_summary: Continued emphasis on Eio compatibility and opam sandbox build robustness while adopting newer liburing/io_uring capabilities.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- Care to preserve downstream Eio compatibility when evolving the API surface, as seen in the `FLAGS.t` revert to keep the type's integer representation available ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159), [ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)).
- Attention to opam's sandboxed build environment, with a second `/tmp`-related build fix landing this week after the liburing 2.15 vendor update exposed the issue ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
- Incremental exposure of newer Linux `io_uring`/`liburing` capabilities at the OCaml layer, from per-operation `RWF` flags to the BPF filter header pulled in with liburing 2.15 ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)).
latest_week.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy shipped RWF flags, liburing 2.15, and the release; Thomas Leonard fixed a build bug; Stéphane Glondu raised vendoring concerns.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented the `Rw_flags` API for per-operation read/write flags ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), updated the vendored `liburing` to 2.15 including the new BPF filter header ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), published the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release, and responded to [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu)'s vendoring concerns on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the liburing 2.15 update and caught an opam sandbox build failure, fixing the vendored `configure` test's use of `/tmp` ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)), restored `FLAGS.t`'s private-int representation to avoid breaking Eio ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), and commented on the vendoring discussion in [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened the discussion on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156) requesting a way to build without vendored `liburing`, explaining the Debian packaging constraint that motivated the request.

latest_week.brief_summary: Eio gained file-ownership (`chown`) and pseudoterminal (`Pty`) support, plus a Windows fd-tracking fix and a `Pool.use` signature change.
latest_week.new_features_summary: New `Path.chown`/`Eio_unix.Pty` APIs, a `login_tty` fork action, a Windows poll-set fix, and a `Pool.use` argument reorder.
latest_week.new_features:
- Added `Eio.Path.chown` for changing file/symlink ownership, implemented across the `posix`, `linux` and `windows` backends, by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) with follow-up flag fixes from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864))
- Introduced `Eio_unix.Pty` for creating and controlling **pseudoterminals**, including window-size get/set and a `Tc` module for termios attributes (`getattr`/`setattr`/`drain`/`flush`/`flow`), plus a `Fork_action.login_tty` action so a child process can be given a pty as its controlling terminal, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531))
- Fixed an **`eio_windows`** scheduler bug where a file descriptor being watched for both read and write, then switched to only one, was left registered in the other poll set, causing the high-CPU busy-loop reported in [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837); fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861))
- Reordered the optional `?never_block` argument in `Eio.Pool.use` to come before the pool argument, a **breaking API change** that makes it obvious to the compiler when the argument is unset, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#747](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/747))
- Raised the minimum `uring` dependency to `2.15.0+` to unlock newer functionality, landed directly on `main` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([8b7b4ed](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/commit/8b7b4edebc359d98f9512f4045b8f652985c4b77), tracked in [#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867))
- Added ownership test coverage to `tests/fs.md` covering `chown` on files, hard links and symlinks with `~follow`, and a new `tests/pty.md` walking through pty creation, window sizing, termios attributes and spawning a child with a pty as its controlling terminal ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531))
latest_week.activity_summary: Two PRs opened this week remain unmerged (uring bump, statx-based fstat), while chown, pty and Windows-poll work landed.
latest_week.activity:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867) to bump the minimum `uring` version to `2.15.0+` and [#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865) to switch Linux `fstat` to use `statx` so that `blksize`/`blocks` are populated for disk-usage calculations, both still open at week's end
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866) so that `Net.getaddrinfo` reports the underlying resolver error instead of silently returning an empty list, fixing the too-many-open-files case from [#776](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/776) and adding `AI_ADDRCONFIG` behaviour on the `linux`/`posix` backends, under review from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- The `chown` follow/nofollow flag bug was fixed twice in parallel: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [#863](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/863) correcting the `AT_SYMLINK_FOLLOW`/`AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW` mixup spotted by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) in [#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), then closed it in favour of [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s smaller fix in [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864), which also added Linux and cross-platform ownership tests
- The long-running **pseudoterminal** effort ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), open since 2023-05-28, was merged this week after review from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), fixing OpenBSD build issues and FD-leak edge cases along the way
- The Windows high-CPU regression reported in [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) by [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) for a bidirectional TCP proxy was fixed and closed via [#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)
latest_week.discussions_summary: Discussion continued on unexplained io_uring IO-pressure reporting and a proposal for standard temp-directory helpers.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [Marijan Petričević](https://github.com/marijanp) reported persistently high `/proc/pressure/io` readings for an otherwise idle `io_uring`-backed service in [#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862); [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) pointed to background reading on how the kernel accounts for `iowait` with `io_uring`, and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggested comparing against the `posix` backend to isolate the cause
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding `Stdenv.tmp` and `Path.with_temp_dir`/`open_temp_dir` helpers for managing temporary directories in [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831); [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) agreed something is needed but suggested it could live directly under `Eio_unix` rather than `Stdenv`, since it doesn't need the same access-restriction treatment as other capabilities
latest_week.trends_summary: Filesystem-metadata coverage (chown, statx) and terminal/process control are both expanding, alongside continued Windows-backend hardening.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- Filesystem metadata APIs are being filled out to match OCaml's `Unix` module, with `chown` landing this week ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864)) and a `statx`-based `fstat` for `blksize`/`blocks` under review ([#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865)), tracked against the broader missing-operations list in [#510](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/510)
- Process and terminal control is deepening with the new `Pty` module and `login_tty` fork action ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), extending beyond plain process spawning
- The Windows backend continues to receive targeted reliability fixes for its `select`-based scheduler, following the read/write poll-set bug fix in [#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)
latest_week.contributors_summary: Core maintainers avsm and talex5 drove chown, pty and Windows-poll work, with community reports from marijanp, z-rui and vog shaping the queue.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) authored and merged the pseudoterminal/`login_tty` support ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), fixed the `chown` symlink-flag bug and added cross-platform tests ([#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864)), opened the `uring` minimum-version bump and the `statx`-based `fstat` change ([#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867), [#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865)), and reviewed [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s `chown` PR and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s `getaddrinfo` error-reporting PR, and commented on the `io_uring` IO-pressure report ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866), [#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) merged the `chown` support and rebased/adjusted it for optional `uid`/`gid` ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)), reviewed and merged the pty work ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), fixed the Windows read/write poll-set bug ([#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)), reordered `Pool.use`'s optional argument ([#747](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/747)), opened and then closed a `chown` flag fix in favour of a smaller alternative ([#863](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/863)), opened the `getaddrinfo` error-reporting PR ([#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866)), and discussed `io_uring` IO-pressure accounting and the temp-directory proposal ([#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862), [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the original `chown` implementation for `eio_posix` and `eio_linux` ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781))
- [@marijanp](https://github.com/marijanp) filed a report of high `io_uring`-related IO pressure on an idle service ([#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862))
- [@z-rui](https://github.com/z-rui) reported the Windows high-CPU two-way-copy bug with a minimal reproduction, which was fixed this week ([#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed temporary-directory helper APIs ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
